title: Peltophorus goaensis
taxonomy: Peltophorus goaensis belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legume or pea family. It is further classified under the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Millettieae. At the genus level, it is classified as Peltophorus, a genus of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Asia. The species P. goaensis is a type of deciduous tree that is endemic to the Western Ghats of India. The classification of P. goaensis is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis.
uses: Peltophorus goaensis is a plant species that has garnered interest in its potential medicinal properties. Research suggests that the plant contains various bioactive compounds, which may be beneficial in treating certain health conditions. However, further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent. benefits: Peltophorus goaensis has been found to have various health benefits. Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and bark have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions. properties: Peltophorus goaensis has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various therapeutic properties. constituents: Peltophorus goaensis is reported to contain several active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits.
